# unch v0.2.0

`v0.2.0` moves `unch` from manual annotation prefixes to Tree-sitter-first symbol indexing. This release changes the search data model and on-disk index schema, so upgrading requires a local reindex and one successful CI republish for repositories that use remote index sync.

## Highlights

- replaces prefix-first indexing with Tree-sitter extraction for top-level symbols in Go, TypeScript, JavaScript, and Python
- indexes declarations and attached documentation on supported languages without requiring `@search:` or `@filectx:` prefixes
- switches search storage to symbol-oriented records with metadata such as kind, name, qualified name, signature, documentation, and body context
- keeps legacy prefix parsing as a fallback path for unsupported files and parser failures instead of removing it outright
- makes remote sync tolerate legacy published indexes during the schema transition instead of failing on the old database format
- splits the `searcher` workflow into dedicated `index` and `publish` jobs and adds manual workflow inputs for rebuild and publish control

## Breaking changes

- `index.db` is not schema-compatible with `v0.1.x`
- every existing local checkout must rebuild its index with `unch index`
- repositories that use remote CI snapshots need one successful `searcher` run after upgrading so CI can republish a compatible remote index
- on Tree-sitter-supported files, results now resolve to declaration lines rather than annotation lines

## Upgrade notes

- run `unch index --root .` after upgrading any existing checkout
- if the repository is bound to remote CI, rerun `searcher` once before expecting `unch remote sync` to restore the latest snapshot again
- existing prefixed comments can stay in the repository, but on supported languages they are no longer required for indexing
- after the first post-upgrade CI rebuild, remote sync resumes against the new published schema
